topic: Being an Effective Consumer of Food
subject: food-technology

items.q: Are 'buy one get one free' offers always good value?
items.correct: Not always; they can encourage buying more than needed, leading to waste.
items.misconception: They are always the cheapest choice with no downsides.
items.why: Students focus on the apparent saving and overlook waste and over-purchasing.

items.q: What does unit pricing allow a shopper to do?
items.correct: Compare the price per standard measure across different pack sizes.
items.misconception: See only the brand's recommended retail price.
items.random_wrong: Find out the food's calorie content.
items.why: Learners confuse unit pricing with total price or nutrition rather than a per-measure comparison.

items.q: If a food product is faulty, what right does a consumer have?
items.correct: The right to a refund, repair or replacement under consumer law.
items.misconception: No right at all once the item has left the shop.
items.random_wrong: The right to free items for life.
items.why: Students wrongly believe leaving the shop ends their consumer rights.

items.q: 'Buy one get one free' promotions can cause problems because they may encourage:
items.why: It's Over-buying and food waste. “Better nutrition” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Unit pricing helps consumers by showing the:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Price per standard measure. “Brand's history” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: If food is faulty or misdescribed, consumers have a right to a:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Refund. “Free advert” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Making an 'informed choice' about food means relying mainly on:
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Accurate label and price information. “Television adverts” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
